title: Consult metric (per logical unit, R29)
description: Per-logical-unit consult signals derived MECHANICALLY from the U6/U8/U9 audit artifacts (answers, chain, aborts) — never from a separate logging path and never from transcripts. The roll-up (scripts/consult-metrics.mjs) computes these so metrics cannot diverge from the audit trail (reconstructable-from-disk, R23/R29).

logical_unit.description: The logical unit these signals roll up (one chain of physical children).

decision_changed.description: True iff the answer differed from the asker's recommendation (the answer changed the call rather than rubber-stamping it). Derived: ask.recommendation != answer.answer for the acted-on answer.

external_fetch.description: True iff the answer's basis was after-reading-code or after-web (the lead did real external work, not answered-from-ask/assumed). Derived from answer.basis.

contradiction_caught.description: True iff an evidenced push-back was accepted (a contradiction was caught). Derived: a chain entry outcome=pushed-back whose subsequent answer revised rather than reaffirmed.

reopened_settled_decision.description: True iff a continuation re-opened an already-settled decision (a fresh-amnesia signal). Derived: a chain entry acting on an answer whose ask duplicates an earlier resolved ask in the same logical unit.

repeated_predecessor_exploration.description: True iff a continuation repeated its predecessor's exploration (fresh-amnesia, not fresh-witness). Derived from the U5 repeated-reread / no-progress signal carried in the abort/chain record.

consult_tokens.description: Cumulative tokens spent on this logical unit's consult chain (sum of per-child consult token counts where recorded; 0 when unrecorded).

consult_latency_ms.description: Wall-clock latency of the consult chain in milliseconds (last answered_at minus the first ask created_at where timestamps exist; 0 when unrecorded).

human_interruption.description: True iff this logical unit reached the human (an answer scope=human-level or an abort/residual that surfaced upward).
